items.week_context: The Nine Days and Tisha b'Av 5782 (standalone halachic guide, no weekly zmanim block)
items.occasion: Nine Days begin (Rosh Chodesh Av); fast postponed since 9 Av is Shabbos
items.label: NOTE
items.verbatim: All the restrictions and limitations of the "Nine Days" commence on Rosh Chodesh (this year on Thurs evening 28 July) and continue until after the end of the actual fast. This year, with the date of 9 (Tisha) b'Av being on a Shabbos, the fast is postponed to 10 Av.

items.week_context: The Nine Days and Tisha b'Av 5782
items.occasion: Nine Days - laundering/mikveh allowance on Rosh Chodesh Av Friday and Erev Shabbos Chazon
items.label: NOTE
items.verbatim: A person who washes with hot water and/or toivels himself in the mikveh every Friday may wash with hot water without soap (others do permit soap) and toivel himself on this Friday (Rosh Chodesh Av) and also next Friday, Erev Shabbos Devarim/ Chazon.

items.week_context: The Nine Days and Tisha b'Av 5782
items.occasion: Motzaei Shabbos 2 Av - Havdalah given to a child
items.label: NOTE
items.verbatim: On motzaei Shabbos 2 Av (this year 30 July) we make Havdalah on wine, but we give the wine to a child who is mature enough to appreciate the importance of making a brocha before drinking, but is not mature enough to appreciate the obligation to mourn over Yerushalayim. If there is no such child available, the person making Havdalah should drink the wine.

items.week_context: The Nine Days and Tisha b'Av 5782
items.occasion: Shabbos Chazon (9 Av) - Torah study restrictions from chatzos
items.label: NOTE
items.verbatim: Shabbos Devarim, Chazon: this year it is on 9 Av: All the restrictions that apply to Torah study on a regular erev Tisha b'Av apply on this Shabbos as well, from chatzot hayom (this year at 12:01pm) onwards. We may study only the books of Eichah and Iyov, the unfavourable prophecies in the book of Yirmeyahu, Midrash Eichah and the description of the churban as recorded in the Talmud Maseches Gittin 55b. The activities prohibited on Tisha b'Av that are private in nature are prohibited on this Shabbos as well.

items.week_context: The Nine Days and Tisha b'Av 5782
items.occasion: Shabbos before the Fast - seudah shlishis allowance
items.label: NOTE
items.verbatim: We may eat meat and drink wine at the meal on Shabbos afternoon before the Fast. However, one should not feast with company.

items.week_context: The Nine Days and Tisha b'Av 5782
items.occasion: Eating cutoff before the Fast begins
items.label: Eating cutoff (shkia)
items.verbatim: All eating and drinking must be finished by shkia (sunset), this year at 5:19pm. Benching may be after shkia.

items.week_context: The Nine Days and Tisha b'Av 5782
items.occasion: Motzaei Shabbos 9 Av - start of Fast observance
items.label: Motzaei Shabbos marker
items.verbatim: At motzaei Shabbos 9 Av (5:58pm), say "Baruch hamavdil bein kodesh l'chol". Then put on non-leather shoes.

items.week_context: The Nine Days and Tisha b'Av 5782
items.occasion: Tisha b'Av - Maariv seating (low chairs)
items.label: Maariv (seating note)
items.verbatim: In the synagogue or at home for Maariv, we sit on low chairs/stools or cushions. The shule furniture is not suitable and it will not be available, so bring your own.

items.week_context: The Nine Days and Tisha b'Av 5782
items.occasion: Tisha b'Av - beracha over a flame after Maariv
items.label: NOTE
items.verbatim: After Maariv, we say the beracha "…Borei me'orei ha'esh" over a flame. However, Havdalah is not said.

items.week_context: The Nine Days and Tisha b'Av 5782
items.occasion: Tisha b'Av - Megillas Eichah and Kinos after Maariv
items.label: Megillah/Kinos
items.verbatim: We continue to sit on low chairs or cushions and read Megillas Eichah and selected Kinos, then say "Ve'Atah Kadosh…", a full kaddish, Aleinu and kiddush yatom.

items.week_context: The Nine Days and Tisha b'Av 5782
items.occasion: Fast day morning washing restriction
items.label: NOTE
items.verbatim: In the morning of the Fast day (this year on 10 Av / Sun 7 Aug), upon rising from sleep, a person should wash only his fingers, and not the entire hand. If he has sleep around his eyes, and he would normally wash it off, he may do so on this occasion as well. We do not say the beracha "…she'asah li kal tzarki" until the next morning.

items.week_context: The Nine Days and Tisha b'Av 5782
items.occasion: Fast day Shacharis changes
items.label: Shacharis (Fast day)
items.verbatim: Shacharis: no tallis or tefillin, and we do not kiss the tzizis of the tallis katan. We say Kinos after the Haftorah. It is customary for each person to say Megillas Eichah individually after the Kinos. We do not say the Tehillim of the day or "Ein Kelokenu…" until the afternoon.

items.week_context: The Nine Days and Tisha b'Av 5782
items.occasion: Fast day - restrictions until chatzos
items.label: NOTE
items.verbatim: Until chatzot hayom (this year at 12:01pm) we don't do any work that involves some time or concentration. Similarly, we do not sit on regular chairs or prepare food for the evening meal until after chatzot hayom.

items.week_context: The Nine Days and Tisha b'Av 5782
items.occasion: Fast day - tallis/tefillin resume, Mincha Gedolah
items.label: Mincha (Fast day)
items.verbatim: Before Mincha: from chatzot hayom (this year at 12:01pm) and onwards we may put on tallis and tefillin saying the appropriate berachos. Then we say the Shema, shir haYom, Ein k'elokeinu, the portion of Tehillim for the day and any shiurim which normally follow Shacharis. That concludes the omissions made in Shacharis. From Mincha Gedolah (this year 12:29pm) we may start saying the korbonos for Mincha, then continue with Krias haTorah and Haftorah, followed by Shmonei Esrei etc.

items.week_context: The Nine Days and Tisha b'Av 5782
items.occasion: Fast day - Rabbeinu Tam tefillin after Mincha
items.label: After Mincha (Fast day)
items.verbatim: After Mincha: we put on Rabbeinu Tam tefillin and say the usual passages.

items.week_context: The Nine Days and Tisha b'Av 5782
items.occasion: End of the Fast
items.label: Maariv / end of Fast / Havdalah
items.verbatim: Ending the Fast: at 5:46pm. We daven Maariv, wash negel vasser (no beracha), wash our faces and rinse our mouths. We can wear leather shoes again. We make Havdalah over wine: we say "…Borei pri hagafen" and the Havdalah beracha, then drink the wine. We don't use a candle or spices.

items.week_context: The Nine Days and Tisha b'Av 5782
items.occasion: End of the Fast - Kiddush Levanah
items.label: Kiddush Levanah
items.verbatim: Make Kiddush Levanah.

items.week_context: The Nine Days and Tisha b'Av 5782
items.occasion: Meat/wine restriction continues until 11 Av morning
items.label: NOTE
items.verbatim: We do not eat meat or drink wine until the morning of 11 Av (Mon 8 Aug). However, the other restrictions of the Nine Days are now removed.

items.verbatim: Rosh Chodesh Tammuz: from Tues night to Thurs evening. Gimmel Tammuz is on Shabbos. Special events planned.

items.verbatim: 17 Tammuz: This year it is on Shabbos, so the Fast is postponed to the next day. Details will be sent out in due course.

items.week_context: Notes for Shabbos 9 Menachem Av and the Fast day postponed to 10 Av (page 2)
items.occasion: Shabbos Chazon Torah-study restriction
items.label: NOTE
items.verbatim: The restrictions that apply to Torah study on Erev Tisha b'Av should be applied on this Shabbos as well. It is proper to study the book of Iyov, the unfavourable prophecies in the book of Yirmeyahu, the Midrash Eichah and the like.

items.week_context: Notes for Shabbos 9 Menachem Av and the Fast day postponed to 10 Av (page 2)
items.occasion: Seudah shlishis allowance before the Fast
items.label: NOTE
items.verbatim: Being Shabbos, one may eat meat and drink wine at the meal before the Fast. However, one should not feast with company.

items.week_context: Notes for Shabbos 9 Menachem Av and the Fast day postponed to 10 Av (page 2)
items.occasion: Eating cutoff (Shkia) - start of Fast
items.label: Eating cutoff (shkia)
items.verbatim: On Shabbos 9 Menachem Av, all eating and drinking must be finished by shkia (sunset), when the Fast commences. This year the time of shkia is at 5:19pm. Benching may be after shkia.

items.week_context: Notes for Shabbos 9 Menachem Av and the Fast day postponed to 10 Av (page 2)
items.occasion: Motzaei Shabbos 9 Av marker
items.label: Motzaei Shabbos marker
items.verbatim: At Motzaei Shabbos 9 Av (this year at 5:58pm), say "Baruch hamavdil bein kodesh l'chol". Then put on non-leather shoes.

items.week_context: Notes for Shabbos 9 Menachem Av and the Fast day postponed to 10 Av (page 2)
items.occasion: BYO low chair/stool for Maariv and Kinos
items.label: NOTE
items.verbatim: BYO: For Maariv and Kinos on Motzaei Shabbos, and also the next morning, please bring a low chair/stool or cushion to sit on. The shule furniture is not suitable and will not be available.

items.week_context: Notes for Shabbos 9 Menachem Av and the Fast day postponed to 10 Av (page 2)
items.occasion: Fast day - regular chairs permitted after chatzos/Kinos
items.label: NOTE
items.verbatim: On the Fast day, after Chatzot hayom and completing the Kinos, we may sit on regular chairs.

items.week_context: Notes for Shabbos 9 Menachem Av and the Fast day postponed to 10 Av (page 2)
items.occasion: Fast day - preparation for Mincha
items.label: NOTE
items.verbatim: Prior to Mincha on the Fast day: put on tallis and tefillin with their respective brochos. Say the Shema, followed by the parts of Shacharis omitted during Shacharis: Song of the Day, Kaveh, Ein Kelokeinu etc, and Tehillim. It is customary for each person to read the book of Eichah for themselves.

items.week_context: Notes for Shabbos 9 Menachem Av and the Fast day postponed to 10 Av (page 2)
items.occasion: Fast day - Mincha order
items.label: Mincha
items.verbatim: Mincha: beginning with korbonos and ketores as usual, then Ashrei etc.

items.week_context: Notes for Shabbos 9 Menachem Av and the Fast day postponed to 10 Av (page 2)
items.occasion: Fast day - Rabbeinu Tam tefillin after Mincha
items.label: After Mincha
items.verbatim: After Mincha: we put on Rabbeinu Tam tefillin and say the usual passages.

items.week_context: Notes for Shabbos 9 Menachem Av and the Fast day postponed to 10 Av (page 2)
items.occasion: End of the Fast - Nine Days restrictions lifted except meat/wine
items.label: NOTE
items.verbatim: After the end of the Fast: Having completed all the requirements of Tisha b'Av, the restrictions of the Nine Days are now permitted, except for meat and wine, which are not permitted until the morning.

items.week_context: Notes for Shabbos 9 Menachem Av and the Fast day postponed to 10 Av (page 2)
items.occasion: Break-fast refreshments, sponsored, with yahrzeit dedication
items.label: NOTE (sponsorship/announcement)
items.verbatim: Breaking the fast of Tisha b'Av: Refreshments at Tzemach Tzedek after Maariv, sponsored by Gershon & Yona Gunsberger in honour of the members of their families who perished al kiddush Hashem, and also in honour of Gershon's alte-zeida Yisroel ben Avraham Kalman haLevi, whose yahrzeit is on Tisha b'Av.

items.week_context: Notes for Shabbos 9 Menachem Av and the Fast day postponed to 10 Av (page 2)
items.occasion: Nightly Siyum through 15 Menachem Av
items.label: NOTE (announcement)
items.verbatim: Nightly Siyum: Until 15 Menachem Av, there will be a siyum each night after Maariv. Come and daven with a minyan and hear a siyum.
